purpose: Horizontal tab bar for the IDE editor area. Reads ideOpenFiles[workspaceId] and ideActiveFileId[workspaceId] from AppContext. Each tab shows file.name, an optional dirty dot (file.isDirty), and a close button. Click on tab body dispatches IDE_SWITCH_FILE; click on close (with stopPropagation) dispatches IDE_CLOSE_FILE — confirms via window.confirm() if file.isDirty. Active tab uses scrollIntoView ref callback to keep itself visible when files are switched programmatically (e.g., from search/debug navigation).
gotchas: Dirty-close confirmation uses window.confirm() — synchronous and blocks the UI; not testable via async flows.

The close button is a <span role='button'> nested INSIDE a <button> — invalid HTML (button-in-button) but works in practice; click handler calls e.stopPropagation() to prevent bubbling to the parent tab's switch handler.
scrollIntoView ref callback runs on every render of the active tab — could fire repeatedly if parent re-renders frequently; behavior 'smooth' adds noticeable animation.
openFiles is keyed by workspaceId — passing the wrong workspaceId silently shows 'No open files'.
patterns: Per-workspace open-files state lives in AppContext (not local) — switching workspaces preserves each one's tabs.
Active-tab ref callback for auto-scroll-into-view (only set on isActive tab).
Dirty indicator is a CSS-only dot via 'editor-tab-dirty' class with bullet character (visual only, not text).
conventions: CSS class 'editor-tab' base + ' active' modifier when active.
